svg.title: Insert Null Values to the Employee Table and Verify the Result

section.data-narration: This is where we introduce the topic of inserting null values into the employee table.
section.text: Insert Null Values to the Employee Table and Verify the Result

section.data-narration: This section explains what null values are and how they are represented in a database.
section.text: Null values are represented by a special symbol, typically represented by three consecutive asterisks (***) or by the word "NULL".

section.data-narration: This section explains the two types of null values: NOT NULL and NULL.
section.text: There are two types of null values: NOT NULL and NULL.
NOT NULL specifies that a column cannot contain null values.
NULL specifies that a column can contain null values.

section.data-narration: This section shows how to insert null values into the employee table using SQL.
section.text: Inserting Null Values into the Employee Table
INSERT INTO Employee (Employee ID, Name, Age, Department) VALUES (4, 'Mike', NULL, 'Sales'), (5, 'Emily', 28, 'IT'), (6, NULL, NULL, 'HR');

section.data-narration: This section shows how to verify the result of inserting null values into the employee table.
